content: A vertically stacked set of interactive headings that reveal or hide associated sections of content. Supports controlled and uncontrolled modes.

Props for Accordion:

- children (required): React.ReactNode — AccordionItem elements to render inside the accordion.
- arrowPosition: 'start' | 'end' — default: 'start' — Position of the expand/collapse arrow icon.
- className: string — CSS class for the root element.
- defaultValue: string | string[] — Initially expanded item(s) for uncontrolled usage.
- disabled: boolean — default: false — Disables all accordion items.
- keepMounted: boolean — default: false — Keep collapsed item content mounted in the DOM.
- multiple: boolean — default: false — Allows multiple items to be expanded simultaneously.
- onUpdate: (value: string | string[]) => void — Callback fired when expanded state changes.
- qa: string — data-qa attribute for testing.
- size: 'm' | 'l' | 'xl' — default: 'm' — Size of accordion items.
- style: React.CSSProperties — Inline styles for the root element.
- value: string | string[] — Controlled expanded item value(s).
- view: 'solid' | 'top-bottom' — default: 'solid' — Visual style of the accordion border.

  <Accordion.Item value="b" title="Item B">Content B</Accordion.Item>
</Accordion>

Props for Flex: - direction: AdaptiveProp<'flexDirection'> — `flex-direction` property - grow: true | React.CSSProperties['flexGrow'] — `flex-grow` property - basis: React.CSSProperties['flexBasis'] — `flex-basis` property - shrink: React.CSSProperties['flexShrink'] — `flex-shrink` property - alignContent: AdaptiveProp<'justifyContent'> — `align-` properties - alignItems: AdaptiveProp<'alignItems'> - alignSelf: AdaptiveProp<'alignSelf'> - justifyContent: AdaptiveProp<'justifyContent'> — `justify-` properties - justifyItems: AdaptiveProp<'justifyItems'> - justifySelf: AdaptiveProp<'justifySelf'> - centerContent: true — Shortcut for: ```css justify-content: center; align-items: center; ``` - wrap: true | React.CSSProperties['flexWrap'] — `flex-wrap` property If value equals `true`, add css property `flex-wrap: wrap`; - inline: boolean — display: inline-flex; - gap: Space | MediaPartial - gapRow: Space | MediaPartial - space: Space | MediaPartial — @deprecated - use native gap property Space between children. Works like gap but supports in old browsers. Under the hoods uses negative margins. Vertical and horizontal directions are also supported --- instead of ~imperfection of the world~ browser compatibility for margins between layout components used negative margins there is passible issues with `background-color` css property and others that depends of current block position. Use in this situations wrappers. In future version this issues will be avoided during flex `gap` properties ```tsx // wrong // right
```

The TableColumnSetup component provides a user interface for configuring table column visibility and order. It displays a button that opens a popup with a list of available columns, allowing users to show/hide columns and optionally reorder them via drag and drop.

- disabled: boolean — default: false — Disables the switcher button.
- getItemTitle: (item: TableColumnSetupItem) => React.ReactNode — Function to get item title.
- hideApplyButton: boolean — default: false — When true, changes are applied immediately; when false, changes require clicking Apply button.
- popupPlacement: PopupPlacement — Popup placement relative to trigger.
- popupWidth: number | 'fit' — Width of the popup.
- renderSwitcher: (props: SwitcherProps) => React.ReactElement | undefined — Custom render function for the switcher button.
- showStatus: boolean — default: false — Shows selected/total columns count in switcher button.
- sortable: boolean — default: true — Enables drag and drop sorting of columns.

A simple text tip that uses its child node as an anchor. This component accepts only text content and may be an excellent alternative to the browser's title attribute with its small size and long appearance delay.

Props for Tooltip:

- children (required): React.ReactElement | ((props: Record<string, unknown>, ref: React.Ref<HTMLElement>) => React.ReactElement) — Anchor element for the Tooltip.
- className: string — class HTML attribute.
- closeDelay: number — default: 0 — Number of ms to delay hiding the Tooltip after the hover ends.
- container: HTMLElement — default: document.body — DOM element to which component is mounted via Portal.
- content: React.ReactNode — Content that will be shown in the Tooltip.
- disabled: boolean — Prevent the Tooltip from opening.
- disablePortal: boolean — default: false — Disables using Portal.
- offset: number — default: 4 — Tooltip offset from its anchor.
- onOpenChange: (open: boolean, event?: Event, reason?: string) => void — Callback to handle open state change.
- open: boolean — Controlled open state.
- openDelay: number — default: 1000 — Number of ms to delay showing the Tooltip after the hover begins.
- placement: string | string[] — default: 'bottom' — Tooltip position relative to its anchor.
- qa: string — data-qa HTML attribute, used for testing.
- role: 'tooltip' | 'label' — default: 'tooltip' — The role Tooltip is used for.
- strategy: 'absolute' | 'fixed' — default: 'absolute' — The type of CSS position property to use.
- style: React.CSSProperties — style HTML attribute.
- trigger: 'focus' — Event type that should trigger opening. By default both hover and focus do.
